title: lepisorus scolopendrium
intro: Lepisorus scolopendrium, also known as the walking fern or lizard's tail, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia, and has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Lepisorus scolopendrium include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and boosting the immune system. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
